pattern: The shape of a hidden sequence -- how far its running total swings from its lowest point to its highest -- is fixed by the differences alone, so this becomes a counting problem about how many starting offsets keep that fixed shape inside a window.
hints: Try picking any starting value and building the sequence out with running totals of the differences -- notice the ups and downs relative to each other never change no matter what you picked.
Track the running minimum and running maximum of that cumulative total (starting the walk at zero); the gap between them is the sequence's fixed span, independent of the starting value.
Once you know that span, the number of valid starting values is just the width of the allowed range minus the span, floored at zero when the span doesn't fit inside the range at all.

edge_inputs.description: mixed increases and decreases within a modest window
single difference at the extreme negative value, wide window
single difference at the extreme positive value, wide window
span far exceeds the window, no valid starting value exists
span exactly one less than the window width, exactly one valid start
all-zero differences, every value in the window is a valid start
single-length differences array, smallest legal case

stress.description: random differences with a wide, randomly sized window
